description.ko: 수직으로 드러난 흙의 단면이 퇴적층과 돌, 광물 얼룩을 시간에 새겨진 촉각적 지도처럼 펼쳐 보입니다.
description.en: An upright soil monolith turns sediment, stone, and mineral stains into a tactile atlas of time-carved land.

prompt: TERRA-1, titled "The Pedolith Atlas." A photorealistic close oblique view of an upright soil monolith cut cleanly from an ancient floodplain, its exposed face resembling a hand-drawn topographic map made from real earth. Compact #8B4513 umber subsoil, #D2691E oxidized clay, and #F4A460 sandy silt appear in thin, uneven sediment bands interrupted by rounded gravel pockets, compressed root fossils, mica flecks, and rough stone nodules. Muted #2F4F4F slate mineral staining pools in small depressions and along pressure seams, giving the surface a grounded, time-worn gravity. Fine dust clings to ledges, crumbly grains fall from undercut shelves, and shallow contour ridges ripple across the cut face like elevation lines. Soft low-angle natural light reveals porous soil, rugged rock texture, stratified deposits, and the slow narrative of land built layer by layer; quiet, tactile, earthy, hyper-detailed geological texture study.
